discovery_instructions: Identify what the main item/entity is for this feature (e.g., employee record, document, invitation)
Look for list/table views - the rows represent the primary entity
Check page headers and breadcrumbs for entity naming
Note the singular and plural forms used in the UI

find_trigger: Look for buttons with: + Add, + Create, + New, plus icon
Check top-right corner of the page
Look in empty state messages for create prompts
create.validation: New item appears in list with unique identifier

find_trigger: Click on any row in a list/table
Look for 'View' or eye icon buttons
Check if rows are clickable (cursor changes)
read.validation: Detail panel, modal, or page opens showing full information

find_trigger: Look for Edit button, pencil icon
Check for inline editing (click on field to edit)
Look for status change dropdowns or buttons
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for edit option
update.validation: Changes persist after save, reflected in list view

find_trigger: Look for Delete button, trash icon
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for delete option
Look for bulk delete with checkboxes
Some features use 'Archive' or 'Cancel' instead of delete
delete.validation: Item removed from list or status changed to deleted/archived

instructions: Navigate to the feature's main section using the navigation_map
Take a full page snapshot to understand the layout
Find ALL clickable elements: buttons, links, tabs, dropdowns, icons
Map the navigation structure: tabs, sidebar items, breadcrumbs
Click each tab/sub-section and document what appears
Locate CRUD controls for the primary entity
Check for action menus (three-dot menus, right-click menus)
Document any modals, dialogs, slide-out panels
Identify form fields and input types when forms appear
Look for filters, search, sort, and export options
Check for bulk action controls (checkboxes, select all)
Note any settings or configuration options
discovery_targets: List/table of items with row actions

content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Articles 14313858348433 and 14313807202961 both cover creating and configuring accrual-based leave policies. The first provides step-by-step policy creation workflow; the second explains the calendar vs working day calculation choice. Consolidated into single task covering full policy setup including day type selection.

content_clusters.topic: Unpaid Leave Deduction Tracking and Payroll Integration
content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Article 14244040988689 covers the technical configuration of unpaid leave deduction calculation methods (Basic Only vs Basic + Allowances, calendar vs working days). PRD 1551532037 enriches with payroll integration details and multi-month deduction splitting logic. Consolidated into single task for HR Admin configuring deduction calculation parameters.

content_clusters.topic: Leave Balance Visibility & Historical Data Access
content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Article 37389941485073 covers bulk updating leave balances for multiple employees using Bayzat Sheets. Standalone article with no PRD enrichment. Consolidated into single task for HR Admin bulk operations.
Single ticket describing inability to auto-calculate pro-rated leave encashment for mid-month departures.
Grouped tickets addressing inability to handle shift-based working days and dynamic recalculation when public holidays change.
Single ticket addressing KSA-specific sick leave accrual requirements not supported by platform.

product_context.title: PRD: Unpaid leave deductions
product_context.why_built: Automate unpaid leave deductions by integrating Time Off module with Payroll module. Eliminate manual data transfer between modules when employees take unpaid leave. Automatically calculate and apply salary deductions based on approved unpaid leave requests, reducing administrative overhead and ensuring accurate payroll processing.
key_design_decisions: Deductions are automatically created upon full leave approval (non-manual trigger)
Deductions are non-editable once created to maintain data consistency; adjustments require modifying the original leave request
Multi-month leave is split into separate deductions per calendar month for accurate payroll processing
Closed payroll months cannot receive new deductions; handled via email notification to Payroll Manager
Daily pay formula: (Basic + Allowances) ÷ Total days (calendar or working based on policy)
Email notifications sent to payroll processor when deductions are created

product_context.title: Opportunity Assessment: Unpaid Leave requests
product_context.why_built: Enable employers to track unpaid leave requests and understand salary deductions associated with unpaid leave for each employee per month. Provide visibility into unpaid leave impact on payroll calculations. Support reporting on unpaid leave across the organization.
key_design_decisions: Unpaid leave requests flow through approval hierarchy before deduction creation
System tracks approved unpaid leave days separately from paid leave
Payroll automatically calculates salary deduction based on approved unpaid leave
HR views overview of unpaid leave deductions per employee per month
Organization-wide reporting on unpaid leave impact

product_context.title: Online doctor - Sick Leave Certificates - Opportunity assessment
product_context.why_built: Enable employees to obtain Sick Leave Certificates (SLCs) through online doctor consultations instead of 2-3 hour hospital visits. For HR admins, provide centralized SLC tracking and storage to eliminate the mess of collecting certificates from multiple sources (email, messengers, physical) in different formats. Increase platform usage and stickiness by integrating with Time-Off module.
key_design_decisions: Integration with Time-Off module triggers SLC flow when sick leave is requested
Online consultation costs AED 50 per session (paid via Checkout.com)
Doctor determines SLC eligibility; not all consultations result in certificates
DHA issues official certificates through HAH platform
Platform provides centralized storage and tracking of SLCs for admin access
SLCs automatically shared with employer via platform

detected_integrations.description: Unpaid leave deductions are automatically created in payroll when leave requests are approved. Deductions are calculated based on employee salary (Basic + Allowances) and leave policy configuration. Deductions appear in payroll adjustments and are processed with payroll runs.

Leave policies define the rules for leave accrual, calculation, and deductions. Leave requests trigger approval workflows and, upon approval, create payroll deductions. Leave policy configuration (calendar vs working days, accrual rates) affects leave request processing.

Employee salary data (basic salary and allowances) is used to calculate unpaid leave deductions. Leave policies are assigned to employees through their profiles. Employee structures enable automatic policy assignment.

Leave requests go through hierarchical approval process. All approvers in the hierarchy must confirm before leave is approved and deduction is created. Approval status triggers payroll deduction creation.

Notifications are sent to payroll processor when unpaid leave deductions are created. Employees and approvers receive notifications at each stage of the leave request workflow. Email alerts notify Payroll Manager for closed payroll month deductions.
Leave policy calculations depend on public holiday configurations, but system lacks dynamic recalculation when holidays are updated, causing policy rule misalignment.

Employee Structures enable automatic employee assignment to leave policies. Without configured structures, manual employee selection is required. Work week configuration affects working day vs calendar day calculations.

Payroll month status (open, closed) determines whether unpaid leave deductions can be created. Closed months require manual email notification. Multi-month leave deductions are split across relevant payroll months.

Leave policy settings (unpaid leave deduction, accrual exclusion) affect End of Service (EOS) calculations and job tenure tracking. System currently deducts unpaid leave from service duration per labour law requirements.
Leave calculations require integration with employee shift schedules and working day configurations, but system cannot account for shift-based days-off in leave tracking.
Leave policy payment type (paid/unpaid) impacts EOS duration tracking and final settlement calculations, restricting ability to change policy type after employee assignment.

check_instructions: Navigate to Automations in the left sidebar
Click + Create workflow (or similar create button)
In trigger selection, search for this feature's name
Check if this feature appears as a trigger source
Document any trigger events found (e.g., 'item created', 'status changed')
Check Actions section for actions related to this feature
Look for 'Approval' or 'Pending Approval' status in the feature
Check if items have approval workflow indicators
Look for 'Submit for Approval' buttons or actions
Find approval settings in feature configuration
Document the approval chain (who approves what)
Test approval/rejection flow if test data available
Check for approval history or audit trail

what_to_do.task: Create an Accrual Leave Policy with Calendar or Working Day Calculation
steps: Navigate to Settings > Leaves > Leave Policies
Click 'Add New' to create a new leave policy
Enter policy name and select leave type (e.g., Annual Leave)
Define allowance conditions based on probation or tenure
Set annual leave days and choose day type: Calendar Days or Working Days
Configure optional settings: enable negative balances, pause accrual during specific leaves, carry forward unused days, restrict applications during probation
Select employees to assign the policy (individual selection or automatic via Employee Structures)
Review policy details and confirm activation
Note: Enabling calendar/working day feature will recalculate existing leave requests and move them to Pending state
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave policy is created and activated. Employees are assigned the policy. Leave accrual begins monthly (total annual days divided by 12). Leave requests are calculated using the selected day type (calendar or working days). Leave balances are tracked and visible in employee profiles.
rules_and_constraints: Leave days accrue monthly (total annual days ÷ 12)
Can set different leave allowances based on probation or tenure
Calendar days count all days including weekends and holidays
Working days only count business days (excludes weekends and public holidays)
Per UAE Labor Law: annual leave can be 30 Calendar Days OR 22 Working Days
Option to restrict employees from editing computed leave days
Automatic assignment requires Employee Structures to be configured; without it, manual employee selection is required
edge_cases: Maximum leave can be limited to contract days
Accrual can be paused during specific leave types
Leave allowances can be customized at individual employee level
Restrictions can be applied to leave applications during probation
Enabling calendar/working day feature recalculates existing leave requests and moves them to Pending state

source_articles.title: How to create accrual leave policies
What is the difference between working day and calendar day leave policies?

what_to_do.task: Create an Unpaid Leave Policy with Basic or Conditional Pay Rates
steps: Navigate to Settings > Leaves > Leave Policies
Click 'Add New' to create a new unpaid leave policy
Enter policy name and select leave type
Choose leave pay rate option: Paid, Partially Paid, or Unpaid
For conditional policies: configure up to 5 pay rate conditions based on number of leave days taken (e.g., first 15 days fully paid, next 30 days half pay, remaining days unpaid)
Set deduction percentage for partially paid leaves if applicable
Configure daily wage calculation (system uses: Salary Amount ÷ 30 days or based on policy day type)
Assign policy to specific employees or select 'Move All' to apply to all employees
Review employee details and edit used days if needed for employees who have already taken leaves
Confirm and save policy
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Unpaid leave policy is created and assigned to employees. When employees take leave matching the policy conditions, payroll automatically calculates and applies the appropriate deduction (full, partial, or none). Deductions appear in payroll adjustments and are processed with the payroll run.
rules_and_constraints: Payroll module must be enabled
Employee salaries must be pre-configured
Leave pay rates can be: Paid (no deduction), Partially Paid (partial salary deduction), or Unpaid (full salary deduction)
Daily wage formula: Salary Amount ÷ Total days (calendar or working based on policy)
Salary = Basic + Allowances
Can add up to 5 pay rate conditions in a single policy
Leave allowance is considered used when leave request is approved
Policy can be edited after creation
Unpaid leave deductions must be calculated daily and set off against the employee's monthly pay
Multiple calculation methods available: Basic Only or Basic + Allowances
Day calculation type affects deduction amount: calendar days result in higher daily rate than working days
Daily rate = (Basic or Basic + Allowances) ÷ (30 calendar days or 22 working days or custom)
Configuration applies to all leave requests under this policy
Leave request requires approval through an approval hierarchy (all approvers must confirm)
Once fully approved, automatic payroll deduction is created
Deduction cannot be edited once created; must adjust leave request to recalculate
Leave spanning multiple months creates separate deductions per calendar month
For closed payroll months, deductions cannot be created; manual email notification sent to Payroll Manager
Deduction amount calculated based on leave policy configuration (Basic Only or Basic + Allowances, calendar or working days)
Deductions are non-editable once created to maintain data consistency
Multi-month leave creates separate deductions per calendar month
Deduction amount = Daily Rate × Number of Leave Days
Daily Rate = (Basic or Basic + Allowances) ÷ (30 calendar days or 22 working days)
Deductions appear in payroll adjustments with type 'Unpaid Leave Deduction'
Deductions are locked and cannot be edited; must adjust leave request to recalculate
edge_cases: Policy only works when payroll module is enabled
Payroll deduction occurs only if leave request meets specified conditions
Default pay rate applies if no specific condition is met
Can edit used leave days for employees who have already taken leaves
Supports conditional pay rates within a single policy (e.g., sick leave: fully paid for first 15 days, half pay for next 30 days, unpaid for remaining 45 days)
Multiple options for daily rate calculation (Basic Only vs Basic + Allowances) affect final deduction amount
Leave spanning multiple months creates separate deductions per calendar month
For closed payroll months, deductions cannot be created and require manual email notification to Payroll Manager
Deductions are non-editable once created; must adjust leave request to recalculate
Leave requests spanning multiple months create multiple deductions (one per month)
Leave requests for closed payroll months require manual email notification to Payroll Manager
Future month deductions are scheduled but email notification is delayed until the month opens
Overlapping deductions show remarks similar to recurring deductions
Users are notified before submission that a deduction will be created
Leave spanning multiple months requires verification that deductions are split correctly across months
Closed payroll months cannot receive new deductions; these are handled via email notification
Future month deductions are scheduled but email notification is delayed until month opens
Deductions must be included in payroll run to be processed

what_to_do.task: Update Leave Policy Allowances in Bulk
steps: Navigate to Settings > Leaves > Leave Policies
Select the leave policy to update
Click 'Edit' icon
Proceed to Employee Details section
Click 'Open Bayzat Sheets' to access bulk update interface
Locate and update the following columns for each employee:
- Annual Allowance: Total leave days allocated
- Pro-rated Days: Partial year allocation
- Days Used Before Setup: Leave taken prior to policy activation
Review changes for accuracy
Save changes (system updates all selected employees simultaneously)
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave policy allowances are updated in bulk for multiple employees. Changes are saved and reflected immediately in employee leave balances. Updated allowances are used for future leave accrual calculations and leave request processing.
rules_and_constraints: Must update specific columns: annual allowance, pro-rated days, and days used before setup
Bulk updates apply to all selected employees in the policy
Changes take effect immediately after saving
Updates must comply with leave policy configuration (accrual rules, maximum limits, etc.)
edge_cases: Bulk updates affect multiple employees simultaneously; verify data accuracy before saving
Pro-rated days apply to employees joining mid-year
Days used before setup account for leave taken before policy activation

source_articles.title: How to add / update leave policy allowance in bulk

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The Bayzat platform prevents modification of core leave policy attributes after initial creation, including accrual frequency, accrual type, carry-over settings, and policy type. HR managers cannot update these fundamental policy parameters without creating entirely new policies, forcing manual workarounds and potential data inconsistency.
what_to_watch_out_for.design_rationale: Architectural constraint to prevent disruption of employee leave balances and historical data integrity
what_to_watch_out_for.documented_workaround: Create new leave policy with desired settings and migrate employees manually

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Leave Balance Visibility & Historical Data Access
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The platform lacks persistent access to leave balance history and granular balance breakdown. When employees are removed from leave policies or policies are deleted, historical leave request data becomes inaccessible. Employees cannot view detailed leave balance breakdowns across multiple policy types, and HR managers cannot access historical leave information after policy removal.

The platform lacks robust mechanisms for handling unpaid leave with precise payroll deduction calculations. There is no automated notification system to flag employees without complete salary configurations when unpaid leave is taken, and payroll reports cannot display partial paid leave scenarios with percentage-based calculations (e.g., KSA 75% sick leave). This creates manual intervention requirements during payroll processing.

The platform cannot automatically calculate pro-rated leave encashment for employees departing before month-end. Employees lose accrued leave days if they depart mid-month, requiring manual intervention to calculate and process partial month encashment.

The platform lacks straightforward mechanisms for leave policy migration between configurations. The leave policy creation process involves multiple sequential steps that could be consolidated, creating unnecessary complexity. Additionally, specific leave policy features require explicit feature flag activation (e.g., 'timeoff-v2' for KSA companies), preventing users from accessing functionality without backend configuration.
what_to_watch_out_for.documented_workaround: Enable feature flags in admin panel; manually recreate policies in new configuration

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The platform lacks the capability to selectively exclude specific leave periods from job tenure calculations. HR managers cannot configure leave policies to exclude extended unpaid leaves or specialized absence arrangements from tenure calculations, preventing accurate representation of complex employment scenarios.

The platform enforces rigid validation rules that prevent leave policy modifications when existing leave requests conflict with proposed changes. The system does not automatically reconcile or adjust leave balances during policy reconfiguration, forcing HR managers to manually resolve conflicts before updating policies.

The leave policy configuration interface includes a redundant activation button for leave encashment policy settings that appears non-functional or confusing. This creates potential user confusion during the policy setup process.

The payroll system uses static salary references instead of dynamic, time-sensitive calculations for unpaid leave deductions, resulting in inaccurate prorated deductions. Additionally, payroll templates lack visibility into unpaid leave counts and salary implications, and administrators cannot edit leave deductions after payroll processing, preventing correction of calculation errors.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Leave Policy Change Data Loss Risk
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The platform lacks rollback or undo mechanisms for leave policy modifications, and deletion workflows lack explicit confirmation warnings. When policies are changed or deleted, employees can be unintentionally unassigned and historical leave balance data may be erased without recovery options.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Leave Calculation for Complex Work Schedules
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The leave management system cannot automatically account for shift-based working days or scheduled days-off in leave calculations, and does not dynamically recalculate leave policies when public holiday configurations are updated. This prevents accurate leave tracking for employees with non-standard work schedules.

The leave policy listing interface lacks sorting and filtering capabilities, employee ID display is missing during policy assignment (creating identification challenges), and documentation on dynamic leave allowance calculations is insufficient. These gaps reduce user efficiency and increase the risk of assigning policies to incorrect employees.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: KSA Regulatory Compliance - Sick Leave Accrual
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The Bayzat platform does not support KSA-specific sick leave regulations that mandate individual, anniversary-based leave accrual cycles. The system defaults to calendar year or hire date-based accrual, which does not meet local regulatory requirements for Saudi Arabian operations.

gaps_identified: People Manager: No reported issues in leave policy configuration or management workflows
persona_coverage.coverage_notes: All personas have at least one reported issue. HR Admin has highest issue density (8 issues) reflecting their role in policy configuration. Finance Manager coverage limited to payroll-related gaps (unpaid leave deductions, encashment calculations).

execution_order: 2. EXPLORATION - Navigate to feature, discover all UI elements using exploration_phase instructions
3. IDENTIFY ENTITY - Determine the primary item/entity using primary_entity discovery
4. CRUD TEST - Test create, read, update, delete for the primary entity
5. WORKFLOW CHECK - Verify if feature has workflow triggers/actions
6. APPROVAL FLOW - Test approval process if present
7. WHAT_TO_DO - Execute each task from what_to_do array, validate expected outcomes
8. WHAT_TO_WATCH_OUT_FOR - Validate known limitations and edge cases
9. DOCUMENTATION - Capture screenshots of every significant state change
10. REPORT - Generate validation report with pass/fail for each task and issue
